text.rule: Before invoking the agent, write a one-paragraph spec containing: goal, acceptance check, files allowed to touch, files forbidden to touch; a chat without this preamble is a vibe-coding session and is forbidden for production code.

Declare a scope fence in the prompt: list the directories/files the agent may modify and instruct it to refuse changes outside that fence; if the agent edits outside the fence, the diff is discarded and the prompt re-issued.
Every agent-authored commit message must contain a "Prompt:" footer summarizing the request and an "Agent:" footer naming the tool/model; commits without these footers fail the pre-commit hook.
Human reads the FULL diff before merge, no exceptions; if the diff is >300 LOC the change is split into smaller chunks until each chunk fits a single review pass.

Any auth, payment, RBAC, migration, or data-loss-capable change triggers a mandatory deep-read pass regardless of LOC size; agent-authored security-sensitive code without deep-read is auto-reverted on detection.

If the agent invents APIs, packages, or function signatures that do not exist (hallucination), record the case in a hallucination log and tighten the prompt with explicit file references before retrying.
No agent-authored change ships to production without at least one assertion test added or updated by the same change; "agent said it works" is not evidence.
text.example: Solo SaaS builder wants to add Stripe webhook handler. Spec: goal = "verify signature, persist event id, dispatch handler"; AC = "duplicate event ids do not double-charge"; scope-in = `src/billing/webhook.py`, `tests/billing/test_webhook.py`; scope-out = everything else. Prompt fences the agent to those two files. Diff comes back at 180 LOC including a test for replay protection. Human reads the full diff, spots a missing constant-time signature compare, asks the agent to fix, re-reads, merges. Commit footer: "Prompt: webhook handler with replay guard / Agent: Claude Code".
